transliteration:
manaḥ-prasādaḥ saumyatvaṃ maunam ātma-vinigrahaḥ |
bhāva-saṃśuddhir ity etat tapo mānasam ucyate ||
literal_meaning: Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-restraint, and purity of intention—this is declared to be mental austerity.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists five cognitive-behavioral targets that calm the default-mode network, reduce rumination, and build an internal locus of control—core skills in any evidence-based therapy.
psychological_state: Mind is trained like a CBT client: notice agitation, label it, choose values-aligned response; gentleness replaces hostility, silence interrupts catastrophic speech, self-restraint blocks impulsive gratification.
trigger_scenario: You’re doom-scrolling at 1 a.m., feel rising self-loathing; instead of posting a rant you close the app, take ten breaths, and jot one kind thing you’ll do tomorrow—practicing manaḥ-prasāda and ātma-vinigraha.
anchor_text: Train your mind to stay calm, kind, quiet, steady, and pure.
